seoTitle: Move a ClickUp Space to Another Workspace (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to move a ClickUp Space to another workspace using the Space Template system, since ClickUp treats workspaces as fully separate environments.
intro: ClickUp keeps every workspace completely isolated, so there's no direct drag-and-drop way to shift a Space from one account to another. Instead, you package the Space as a shareable template, generate a public link, and import it into the destination workspace. This guide walks through that template-based transfer so your folders, lists, and tasks carry over cleanly.

steps.heading: Find the Space you want to transfer
steps.body: Locate the target Space in your left sidebar, since this is the starting point for turning it into a portable template.

steps.heading: Save the Space as a template
steps.body: Open the three-dot menu next to the Space name, go to the Template Center, and choose Save as Template to package all folders, lists, and views.

steps.heading: Name it and enable public sharing
steps.body: Give the template a clear internal title and description, then toggle on public sharing so ClickUp generates an external link you can copy.

steps.heading: Import into the destination workspace
steps.body: Open a new browser tab, sign into your target workspace, paste the shared link, and use it to select where the imported Space should land.

steps.heading: Recreate the Space structure
steps.body: Click Use Template to rebuild the entire Space architecture, including folders, lists, and views, inside the new workspace.

steps.heading: Verify the migration
steps.body: Check that task assignees, custom tags, and folder configurations transferred correctly before removing the original Space.

faq.question: Can I move a ClickUp Space directly between workspaces?
faq.answer: Not directly. ClickUp treats workspaces as isolated environments, so you need to save the Space as a template and import it into the new workspace.

faq.question: Will task assignees carry over automatically?
faq.answer: Assignee mapping depends on whether the same people exist in the destination workspace, so it's worth double-checking assignments after the import.

faq.question: Is the shared template link public to anyone?
faq.answer: Yes, once you toggle public sharing on, anyone with the link can view and import the template, so treat the URL as sensitive until you're done.

faq.question: Does this method keep custom fields and views?
faq.answer: Yes, the Save as Template option is designed to package custom fields, views, and active tasks along with the folder structure.
